category_title: ADHD-diagnosed student who seeks social contact and struggles with exclusion and medication-dependent behavior
minimal_context: The student is transitioning to the playground with his classmates after the lunch bell rings.
dialogue:
TUTOR: The bell just rang for the end of lunch. Everyone, please run out into the playground.

STUDENT: (Forgets to take his medicine and runs out into the playground.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and find something to do with your classmates.

STUDENT: (Grabs his classmates, holds their necks and twists their arms around.)

TUTOR: What is happening over there?

STUDENT: (After a boy tells him to stop and the classmates distance themselves from him, he stands alone and stares into the sky.)

options.A: Constantly repeat modeling, show the student how to interact, and then encourage him to 'have a go' on the playground.
options.B: Give the student a hug, tell him he is a lovely person, and reassure him that he will soon find friends in the class.
options.C: Supervise the playground activity to ensure students follow agreed rules and learn to respectfully take turns.
options.D: Ask an open-ended question to facilitate a collaborative solution with his classmates (e.g., 'How would that work?').
